candidate: STOMATOPOD-HEAD: replace standard multi-head attention with K=16 narrow-band specialist sub-heads, each on a disjoint DCT spectral slice, with no intra-layer cross-channel comparison and delayed aggregator.

calibration_anchors.rank_1: Mantis shrimp biology source page — pure source-domain, no LLM/attention content. Should yield 0/0/0.
calibration_anchors.rank_2: FDW (CVPR 2025) — disjoint Fourier groups in convolution kernels. Same 'frequency-disjoint specialists' artefact in a different operator class. KW partial; semantic moderate-high; functional moderate-high.
calibration_anchors.rank_3: Wavelet-like emergent band-specialist heads in transformers (ACL 2025) — describes emergent specialization (not architectural imposition). KW moderate, sem/func moderate-high.
calibration_anchors.rank_4: Frequency Band Attention Mechanism — per-band heads with independent modules merged via gates. Direct functional twin of candidate; high on all axes.
calibration_anchors.rank_5: FNet — FFT replaces attention entirely; does NOT keep K specialist heads with disjoint bands. Different mechanism class; low functional similarity.
calibration_anchors.rank_6: Spectral-Slice MHA with Disjoint DCT Bands — explicitly K disjoint DCT bands → K specialist heads + late aggregator. Near-identical to candidate's claim; near-maximal scores.
calibration_anchors.rank_7: Band-Disjoint Specialist Heads for Long-Context Transformers — explicitly addresses softmax-temperature interference via band disjointness, matching candidate's hypothesis verbatim.
calibration_anchors.rank_8: Wavelet-Banded MHA with delayed cross-band gating — wavelet variant of the exact STOMATOPOD-HEAD shape (band-disjoint specialists + delayed aggregator).

per_result_judgments.title: Mantis shrimp — Wikipedia
per_result_judgments.rationale: Source-domain biology page. No LLM, attention, DCT, or band-specialist content. Provides only the inspiration, not prior art on the LLM application.

per_result_judgments.title: Frequency Dynamic Convolution (FDW, CVPR 2025)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Snippet describes 'spectral coefficients divided into frequency-based groups with disjoint Fourier indices' — exactly the 'frequency-disjoint specialist' artefact, though in convolution kernels rather than attention heads. Same architectural primitive transferred to a sibling operator. Counts as kw>=2 (frequency-disjoint, spectral groups) and at functional threshold.

per_result_judgments.title: Beyond Position: emergence of wavelet-like properties in Transformers (ACL 2025)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Attention heads exhibiting 'wavelet-like band specialization across frequency' is the same observable phenomenon as STOMATOPOD-HEAD's intended structure. However, this is emergent (post-hoc analysis) rather than imposed architecture, so functional score is below threshold; kw>=2 (attention heads, frequency band specialization) triggers the hit rule.

per_result_judgments.title: Frequency Band Attention Mechanism (2025)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Snippet states 'per-band heads specialize to different band subgroups; outputs of independent band-specific modules merged via learnable gates' — this is essentially STOMATOPOD-HEAD verbatim minus the DCT-specific basis. Hits on all three axes.

per_result_judgments.title: FNet: Mixing Tokens with Fourier Transforms (2021)
per_result_judgments.rationale: FNet replaces self-attention entirely with parameter-free FFT mixing across token AND channel axes. The opposite of STOMATOPOD-HEAD: it actively mixes across channels and has no specialist heads. Shares only 'Fourier transforms in transformers' keyword; functionally distinct.

per_result_judgments.title: Spectral-Slice Multi-Head Attention with Disjoint DCT Bands (2026/03)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Snippet: 'K disjoint DCT bands assigned to K specialist heads; no intra-layer cross-band interaction; merged via late aggregator.' This is literally the candidate's mechanism stated almost word-for-word. Maximal overlap on every axis.

per_result_judgments.title: Band-Disjoint Specialist Heads for Long-Context Transformers (2025/11)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Frequency-disjoint heads explicitly motivated by avoiding 'inter-head softmax-temperature interference' — matches both the architecture AND the specific claimed contribution ('per-channel softmax-temperature decoupling') of the candidate. Direct prior art on the novelty claim.

per_result_judgments.title: Wavelet-Banded Multi-Head Attention (2026/04)
per_result_judgments.rationale: Wavelet-band-disjoint specialist heads with delayed cross-band gating — same STOMATOPOD-HEAD blueprint, using wavelet basis instead of DCT. The 'delayed cross-band' phrasing matches the candidate's 'delayed aggregator stage' content word exactly.

summary: Six of eight results clear the hit threshold, with three (ranks 6, 7, 8) being direct architectural twins of STOMATOPOD-HEAD: K disjoint DCT/wavelet bands assigned to specialist attention heads with no intra-layer cross-band interaction and a delayed aggregator. Rank 7 even matches the candidate's specific softmax-temperature-decoupling claim. The candidate's stated novelty (frequency-disjoint specialists with no intra-layer cross-channel comparison) is firmly established prior art.
